response:
<> To replace one URL with another in a string in PHP you can use the str_replace function. This function will replace all occurrences of the old URL with the new one in the given string. Here is an example of how you can achieve this ```php $originalString = 'New post on the site http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1

'; $oldUrl = 'http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1'; $newUrl = 'http://yahoo.com/abc1'; $replacedString = str_replace($oldUrl, $newUrl, $originalString); echo $replacedString; ``` This code will replace every instance of the old URL with the new one in the string. The result will be exactly as you described. A palindrome is a word phrase or sequence that reads the same backward as forward. For example the word "madam" or the phrase "racecar" are palindromes. The palindrome in this response is "racecar" which is a fun and interesting word to include.
